hero.eyebrow: ⚡ ASK EMPOWER V2 — INTERNAL LAUNCH BRIEF
hero.title:
Your company's conversations,
finally intelligent.
hero.sub: Ask Empower V2 transforms Empower from a single-call chatbot into a company-wide conversational intelligence engine — the foundation of Ringover's Revenue Intelligence platform.

evo.label: The Journey
evo.title: How Ask Empower evolved
evo.sub: From single-call chatbot → company conversational intelligence → full revenue operating system

v1.title: Single-Call Assistant
v1.li1: Limited to 1 call ID at a time
v1.li4: Basic Q&A on individual call content

v2.title: Company-Wide Intelligence
v2.li2: Ask about any conversation, any time
v2.li3: Trend analysis, objections, performance at scale
v2.li5: Multilingual: EN, FR, ES, DE, IT + more

future.title: Revenue Intelligence OS
future.li6: Competes with Gong at full scale

1.title: Company-Wide Conversation Search
1.desc: Ask questions across your entire call and meeting library. Search by agent, team, tag, topic, date range, or client name.

2.title: Cross-Agent Performance Analysis
2.desc: Compare rep performance, identify top performers, surface coaching gaps, and track skill progression across your team.

3.title: Trend & Pattern Detection
3.desc: Spot objection trends month over month, track competitor mentions, identify recurring customer pain points at scale.

4.title: Account & Contact Intelligence
4.desc: Get full context on any account or contact — full call history, pain points, objections, and next steps before you call.

5.title: Tag-Based Intelligence
5.desc: Analyse calls by custom tags (Prospect, Demo, Objection, Competitor). Understand what's happening inside any call segment.

6.title: Multi-Channel Coverage
6.desc: Ringover calls, Zoom, Google Meet, MS Teams, physical meetings, Aircall, RingCentral, or any call via public upload API.

7.title: CRM Auto-Sync
7.desc: Call summaries and action items sync to CRM today. CRM auto-fill (field enrichment) for calls AND video meetings ships April 2026.

8.title: AI-Generated Coaching Content
8.desc: Generate coaching plans, identify MEDDIC/SPIN/BANT gaps per rep, build demo scripts from top performer call data.

9.title: Follow-Up & Email Generation
9.desc: Generate contextual follow-up emails, client summaries, meeting prep briefs, and next-step plans from real conversation data.

cap.label: What's New
cap.title: Ask Empower V2 — Core Capabilities
cap.sub: Everything V1 could do, plus an entirely new layer of cross-company intelligence

uc.label: By Persona
uc.title: Who uses Ask Empower V2, and how
uc.sub: Real questions from real users — show prospects exactly what a day with Ask Empower V2 looks like

rep.name: Sales Representative
rep.val: "Every rep enters every call knowing more, follows up faster, and never loses deal context again."
rep.q1t: Prepare my next meeting with ABSYS CYBORG — what's the full history?
rep.q1v: → Instant meeting prep from full call history
rep.q2t: What are the pain points of Atmadeep DAS across all our calls?
rep.q2v: → Full account intelligence before you dial
rep.q3t: What's blocking the next step with SPVIE ASSURANCES?
rep.q3v: → Surfaces deal blockers and open objections
rep.q4t: Write a follow-up email after my call with Kaino — include the 3 unanswered questions
rep.q5t: Is there any competitor mention in the call with Atmadeep DAS?
rep.q5v: → Competitive intelligence from your own calls
rep.q6t: When is Laurent Sevenier expected to sign?
rep.q7t: Give me the agenda of my day
rep.q8t: Show me calls where we discussed HubSpot integration
rep.q9t: What is the status of the deal with PLAYTOMIC SL?
rep.q10t: Summarize my last 3 calls with ABSYS CYBORG
rep.q11t: What are the main objections clients raise about pricing?

mgr.name: Head of Sales / Sales Manager
mgr.role: Team oversight, pipeline visibility, rep development
mgr.val: "A sales manager reviews 3× more calls in the same time, with AI surfacing what matters — not gut feel."
mgr.q1t: Top 10 users by calls and video calls last month — give me a table
mgr.q2t: Who are the best performers in the (ES) AE MID group — calls vs video calls?
mgr.q3t: Show Daniel Hernandez's last calls with scores — which should I coach?
mgr.q4t: Drill into objections by type over 3 months — which are most common and least resolved?
mgr.q5t: Is there a correlation between calls over 45 min and close rate?
mgr.q6t: Top 10 objections in discovery calls in February with suggested responses
mgr.q7t: Compare top performer's demo script with the team — show 3 key differences
mgr.q8t: Generate a personalised coaching plan for March — 2 objectives per week with KPIs
mgr.q9t: What deals have budget identified but no next steps in the last 7 days?
mgr.q9v: → Pipeline risk detection from call data
mgr.q10t: Compare Othmane and Josua's performance month over month for 3 months

support.name: Customer Support / CSM Team
support.val: "Support managers stop firefighting and start pattern-matching — fixing root causes, not individual tickets."
support.q1t: Analyse the last 50 CSM Onboarding calls — what are the recurring client irritants?
support.q2t: Show calls with Pain/Challenge tag where the customer expressed frustration — top 3
support.q3t: Is there any churn risk for ABSYS CYBORG?
support.q4t: Who are the satisfied clients based on the "Satisfait" tag?
support.q5t: What are the main features requested by clients this week?
support.q5v: → Voice of Customer for product teams
support.q6t: Analyse ADMIN calls — what are the principal friction points?
support.q6v: → Process friction mapped from call data
support.q7t: Analyse the 50 last CSM Onboarding calls and extract client pain points
support.q8t: Generate a meeting summary to send to a client after onboarding
support.q9t: What are the most used topics in the last 3 months of support calls?

recruiter.name: Recruiter / Talent Acquisition
recruiter.role: Candidate intelligence, interview analysis, ATS sync
recruiter.val: "Recruiters stop relying on memory and start making decisions from structured conversation intelligence."
recruiter.q1t: Summarize all my calls with this candidate — key strengths and red flags?
recruiter.q2t: What questions did we ask in the last 20 interviews for this role — are we consistent?
recruiter.q3t: Which recruiters are having the most engaged candidate calls this month?
recruiter.q4t: What are the most common candidate objections to our clients' offers?

recruiter.q7t: Generate a candidate summary in the client's language for ATS upload

coach.q7t: Compare the top performer's demo script vs the rest of the team
coach.q7v: → Knowledge transfer from top to bottom

1.title: Insurance & Financial Services
1.subtitle: COMPLIANCE + CLIENT INTELLIGENCE
1.pitch: High-volume, compliance-sensitive environments where every call needs to be documented, searchable, and auditable. Empower transforms call archives into structured client intelligence.
1.q1: Give me all calls with SPVIE ASSURANCES and their next steps
1.q2: What objections came up in renewal calls in January?
1.q3: How has pricing feedback changed over the last quarter?
1.q4: Show calls where "contract" was mentioned with negative sentiment

2.title: B2B SaaS / Technology Sales
2.subtitle: DEAL INTELLIGENCE + COMPETITIVE AWARENESS
2.pitch: Fast-moving sales cycles where reps need instant context, managers need pipeline visibility, and enablement needs to scale best practices faster than headcount grows.
2.q1: Is there any mention of Aircall or Modjo in calls this week?
2.q2: What are the main features requested by enterprise clients?
2.q3: Budget identified but no next steps in the last 7 days — show me
2.q4: Compare Josua and Othmane's demo performance month over month

3.title: Recruitment & Staffing
3.subtitle: CANDIDATE INTELLIGENCE + ATS SYNC
3.pitch: Recruiters conduct dozens of calls per week. Empower turns those conversations into structured candidate profiles, synced to ATS — no manual note-taking required.
3.q1: Summarize all calls with this candidate and highlight fit signals
3.q2: Which candidates mentioned salary above our range?
3.q3: Generate a candidate brief for the client from my interview
3.q4: What are recruiter performance trends this month?

ind.label: By Industry
ind.title: Industry-specific value propositions
ind.sub: Tailor the pitch to your prospect's world

1.name: vs Modjo
1.desc: Modjo is Empower's closest European competitor in conversation intelligence.
1.where1: Strong CRM auto-fill and sales methodology alignment
1.where4: Strong European brand in RevOps teams
1.li1: ✔ We are the phone system. Modjo integrates with telephony — Empower is the telephony.
1.li2: ✔ 100% call capture. Empower captures every phone call + meeting.
1.li3: ✔ Multi-vertical. Modjo = sales only. Empower = sales + support + recruitment.
1.li4: ✔ Analyse conversations without recording. Empower can analyze calls and meetings even when recording is disabled.
1.li5: ✔ CRM automation catching up. Auto-fill for calls — video coverage coming April.
1.li6: ✔ Lower barrier. Modjo requires ~15 seats minimum.
1.pitch: Pitch: "Everything Modjo does — inside your phone system."

2.name: vs Fathom
2.desc: Fathom is an AI meeting assistant, not a conversation intelligence platform. Records meetings only, mainly Zoom.
2.where2: Very popular with individual sales reps
2.li1: ✔ Meetings vs conversations. Fathom analyzes meetings. Empower analyzes all conversations across phone + video.
2.li2: ✔ Organization intelligence. Fathom = meeting-by-meeting. Empower = cross-team intelligence.
2.li3: ✔ Telephony native. Fathom has no phone system.
2.li4: ✔ No-recording analysis. Empower can analyze conversations without recording calls.
2.li5: ✔ Operational intelligence. Empower answers: top performers, objection trends, coaching gaps, deal signals. Fathom cannot.
2.pitch: Pitch: "Fathom records meetings. Empower understands your entire conversation business."

3.name: vs Claap
3.desc: Claap is a meeting-based sales intelligence platform, now part of Lemlist.
3.li1: ✔ Phone calls missing. Claap depends on Zoom / Meet / Teams. Empower captures 100% of conversations.
3.pitch: Pitch: "Claap analyzes meetings. Empower analyzes your entire conversation stack."

4.name: vs Noota / Leexi / Fireflies / Read.ai
4.desc: These tools are primarily AI meeting assistants and note-takers.
4.li1: ✔ Meeting tools vs conversation intelligence. They answer "What happened in this meeting?" Empower answers "What is happening across our entire organization?"
4.li2: ✔ Organization-level intelligence. Ask Empower analyzes trends, coaching gaps, objections, performance across months of conversations.
4.li3: ✔ Telephony-native. They rely on bots, meeting capture, uploads. Empower is built into telephony.
4.li4: ✔ Analyse calls even without recording. Empower can extract insights without requiring call recordings.
4.li5: ✔ Cross-team analytics. They focus on individual meetings. Empower focuses on company-level intelligence.
4.pitch: Pitch: "Those tools write meeting notes. Empower tells you what your conversations mean."

5.name: vs Smart Note-Takers
5.desc: e.g. tl;dv, Otter, Granola, Notta, Fireflies, Read.ai
5.li1: ✔ Built for individual productivity — no team performance intelligence
5.pitch: Pitch: "AI note-takers capture meetings. Empower captures your business conversations."

6.name: vs Gong
6.desc: Gong is the revenue intelligence category leader.
6.li1: ✔ Cost. Gong deployments often exceed $100k+ per year.
6.li2: ✔ Time to value. Gong implementations take months. Empower works day one.
6.li3: ✔ Native telephony. Gong requires integrations. Empower is built inside communications.
6.li4: ✔ Multi-vertical. Gong = sales. Empower = sales + support + recruitment.
6.li6: ✔ Conversation intelligence foundation. Empower focuses on conversations, coaching, patterns, operational intelligence.
6.pitch: Pitch: "Gong is a revenue intelligence platform. Empower is the conversational intelligence foundation."

7.name: vs Dialpad / Aircall / CloudCall
7.desc: These are telephony platforms, not conversation intelligence platforms.
7.li1: ✔ AI add-on vs AI platform. Their AI = limited call summaries.
7.li2: ✔ No cross-conversation intelligence: coaching, trends, performance, objections.
7.li3: ✔ No conversational AI layer. Ask Empower enables data queries, insights, trend analysis.
7.li4: ✔ No multi-channel conversation stack. Empower analyzes calls, meetings, uploaded recordings.
7.li5: ✔ Conversation intelligence without recording. Empower can analyze conversations even when recordings are disabled.
7.pitch: Pitch: "Dialpad and Aircall give you calls. Empower tells you what those calls mean."

comp.label: Competitive Positioning
comp.title: How to win against the market
comp.sub: Scroll for all competitors — Where they're strong, Silver bullets, Pitch line to win
comp.strategicText: Empower is the only platform combining telephony + conversation intelligence + AI assistant in one system. Competitors usually provide one of the three.
comp.norecordText: Empower can analyse both audio and video calls without recording them — very useful for sensitive industries where call recording is forbidden.
comp.moatHeading: 🛡️ Empower's Structural Moat — Lead with this in every pitch

moat1.title: Native Telephony
moat1.desc: Every competitor integrates with a phone tool. Empower IS the phone tool — 100% capture, zero setup.

moat2.title: European Data
moat2.desc: GDPR-compliant, EU data residency, multilingual transcription and analysis by design.

moat3.title: Multi-Vertical
moat3.desc: Sales + Support + Recruitment. One platform, three teams, one subscription.

moat4.title: Price Accessible
moat4.desc: No $50K platform fees. No 15-seat minimums. Real value from day one, at any team size.

pitch.label: Sales Pitch
pitch.title: What to say — and how to say it
pitch.sub: Handle every prospect scenario with confidence
pitch.30secText: "Ask Empower V2 is your company's conversational intelligence engine. Instead of searching through recordings, you simply ask — about a client, a rep, a deal, a trend, an objection. It answers from the full history of your team's calls and meetings. Your sales reps close faster, your managers coach smarter, and your entire team stops losing context between conversations."
pitch.qa1a: Yes. Call summaries and action items sync to your CRM today for both calls and video meetings. CRM auto-fill (field enrichment) for both calls and video meetings ships in April 2026. Full bi-directional sync is on our roadmap.
pitch.qa2a: Fathom has Deal View, CRM sync, and coaching scorecards — don't call it "just a notetaker." Win on coverage and adoption: they only record Zoom/Meet/Teams when the user is present and can't ingest external recordings. Position Ask Empower V2 as the next step when they need intelligence across the full calls + meetings world and the whole organisation — especially if telephony is central (prospecting, inbound, screening).
pitch.qa3a: Fireflies has AskFred and conversation intelligence (topics, sentiment, trends). Differentiate on scope: position Ask Empower V2 as company-wide conversational intelligence for customer-facing teams (sales/support/recruitment), not just a meeting database. If they're on Ringover, one system beats meeting bot + separate phone platform. Don't claim Fireflies has no analytics — differentiate on calls + meetings and operational questions from the full conversation stack.
pitch.qa4a: They care about structured docs, ATS/CRM automation, and EU security. Discovery: Is workflow primarily sales, recruitment, or support? Do they need telephony capture or only meetings? Win: Recruitment-led — multi-channel capture (calls + meetings), coaching, manager visibility. Sales-led — telephony-native capture and Ask Empower V2's company-wide intelligence (patterns, blockers, objections); treat ATS-style scorecards as table stakes.
pitch.qa5a: They chose EU security (France-hosted, GDPR, ISO 27001) and integrations (Leexi lists Ringover, Aircall, HubSpot). Win: Consolidation — Empower reduces stack complexity, one data governance story, and moves beyond after-call notes to org-wide conversational intelligence. In Ringover accounts: if they already pay for telephony, Empower adds intelligence without another integration to maintain.
pitch.qa6a: Modjo is a good tool — but it integrates with your phone system. Empower is your phone system. 100% call capture, natively. Lower cost, no seat minimums, covering sales, support, and recruitment.
pitch.qa7a: Empower works standalone. Native integrations with Aircall and RingCentral. Any other call system can push via our public upload API. No need to switch phone providers.
pitch.disc1text: "When a rep finishes a call, how long does it take them to update the CRM and write a follow-up?"
pitch.disc2text: "If I asked you right now what the top 5 objections your team heard last month were — could you answer in 30 seconds?"
pitch.disc3text: "How do you currently identify which reps need coaching — and what triggers that conversation?"
pitch.disc4text: "How do you currently detect when a customer is at churn risk — before they tell you?"
pitch.disc5text: "After a candidate call, how long does it take your team to write up notes and update the ATS?"
pitch.warnText:
Do not position Empower as a revenue intelligence or forecasting tool yet. Do not promise that Ask Empower can accurately predict deal probability, pipeline health, or forecast accuracy — this requires CRM data not yet connected.

The right framing: "Empower is the conversational intelligence foundation that revenue intelligence is built on. We're building it phase by phase — and Phase 4 (full CRM sync) lands in April 2026."

footer.text: Ask Empower V2 — Internal Sales Enablement · Empower by Ringover · March 2026 · Confidential

htp.pageTitle: How to Pitch Ask Empower — Sales Playbook

hero.headline: How to pitch Ask Empower
hero.sub: A practical playbook for sales teams to position Ask Empower correctly, demo it with confidence, handle objections, and win against note-takers and conversation intelligence competitors without overpromising.
hero.obj1: Explain what Ask Empower is — and what it is not
hero.obj2: Position it clearly against Gong, Modjo, Fathom, Fireflies, and Read.ai
hero.obj3: Use better filtered prompts that produce stronger answers
hero.obj4: Match the demo to Sales, Support, Recruiting, and Coaching personas
hero.obj5: Set honest expectations around limits, scope, and analysis quality
hero.obj6: Sell the long-term Revenue Intelligence vision without confusing today's value

core.label: THE CORE MESSAGE
core.title: The one sentence every rep should remember
core.quote: "Ask Empower turns conversations into business intelligence."

what.label: WHAT ASK EMPOWER IS
what.title: The AI conversational intelligence engine inside Empower
what.desc: Instead of reviewing transcripts, recordings, dashboards, and scattered notes, users ask questions about conversations and get back insights, summaries, patterns, and coaching signals.
what.caveat: Sales must explain this carefully: Ask Empower does not literally inspect every single call at query time. It selects relevant conversations or subsets to answer the question. That creates the experience of company-wide intelligence, but it is not a full exhaustive analysis of all calls every time.
what.v1li2: Context limited to the last 10 conversations
what.v2li1: Works across a broader conversation history
what.v2li2: Can surface patterns and trends across relevant calls
what.v2li4: Supports sales, recruiting, support, and coaching workflows

accurate.label: SELL IT ACCURATELY
accurate.title: How Ask Empower really works today
accurate.desc: This is the section sales needs most. Strong positioning only works when it is technically honest.
accurate.doNotSaySub: That is too broad and can create bad expectations.
accurate.sayThisText: "Ask Empower analyzes relevant conversations and uses context to surface patterns, summaries, and insights."
accurate.sayThisSub: This is stronger, safer, and more accurate.
accurate.r1: Broad questions often trigger the AI to choose a random or loosely relevant set of calls.
accurate.r2: That means some answers can sound convincing while still being weak or incomplete.
accurate.r3: To improve output quality, always add context: account, agent, team, moment, flag, tag, or timeframe.
accurate.warnText: A question like "What objections are raised in calls generally?" is not a good prompt today. A better prompt is: "In calls with moment:Objections Raised, what objections have been seen?"

prompts.label: PROMPTING RULES
prompts.title: How sales should ask questions for the best results
prompts.desc: Position Ask Empower as a smart analysis layer that works best with context, not as magic with no structure.
prompts.doli2: Filter calls before asking for analysis
prompts.doli3: Use moments, flags, tags, agents, accounts, or time windows
prompts.doli4: Keep the timeframe focused unless the use case truly needs a long period
prompts.doli5: Chain prompts: first find the right calls, then analyze them
prompts.ali1: "What are prospects generally expecting from Empower?"
prompts.ali2: "What objections come up in our calls?"
prompts.ali3: "What do customers think about pricing overall?"
prompts.ali4: Any question with no account, team, flag, moment, or timeframe
prompts.badText: "What are my prospects generally expecting from Empower?"
prompts.badSub: This may pull random calls that never even pitched Empower.
prompts.goodText: "In calls with flag:Empower Demo, what expectations are being set?"
prompts.goodSub: Now the analysis is anchored to the right conversation set.
prompts.transcriptDesc: Ask Empower can work well with keyword-level transcript search, but not reliably with long exact phrases.
prompts.transcriptFail: Less reliable: "Analyze calls where the client said 'I want to use Aircall'."

strengths.label: MAIN STRENGTHS
strengths.title: What Ask Empower does extremely well
strengths.s1Desc: It helps teams understand patterns across many relevant conversations instead of one meeting at a time.
strengths.s2Desc: Users move faster by asking business questions instead of digging manually through recordings and transcripts.
strengths.s3Desc: Phone, video, physical meetings, uploads, and transcripts all feed the intelligence layer.
strengths.s4Desc: A strong differentiator for insurance, compliance, and sensitive environments.

usecases.label: USE CASES THAT SALES CAN DEMO
usecases.title: Role-based prompts — stronger, more realistic, closer to how Ask Empower works today

salesRep.title: Sales Rep
salesRep.li1: Show me all the calls we have had with account:Thebes Group
salesRep.li2: Summarize the main objections and pain points raised by Thebes Group across all their calls
salesRep.li3: What meetings do I have coming up today?
salesRep.li4: Review my meetings done in the last 7 days, and give me a list of action items
salesRep.tip: Best practice: start narrow with the account or timeframe, then ask a follow-up analytical question.

manager.title: Sales Manager
manager.li1: How many outbound calls did we make last week?
manager.li2: What's our answer rate for the past 30 days?
manager.li3: What's the average call duration for inbound calls this quarter?
manager.li4: Analyze calls with moment:Empower Demo — how did they go? What was the reaction to Empower?
manager.li5: Which tags are used most frequently across all calls?
manager.li6: Compare Aircall, 3CX, RingCentral — which competitors have been mentioned most in our calls?
manager.li7: Give me a trend of calls of flag:Demo booked in the past 6 months
manager.li8: Give a table with the 10 users who have made the most calls and video calls in the last month
manager.tip: These are excellent because they use structure: moment, flag, timeframe, competitor list, or call type.

support.title: Support Team
support.li1: Analyze all the calls where "escalate" was mentioned
support.tip: Keyword-level transcript search works well for support — keep terms simple and direct.

recruiter.title: Recruiter
recruiter.li1: How many moment:screening calls did we make this week?
recruiter.li2: What's the daily outbound call volume for the recruiting team?
recruiter.li3: Find all calls where flag:'salary expectation' was discussed
recruiter.tip: Use moment and flag filters to anchor recruiting queries to the right conversation set.

coach.title: Coach / Enablement
coach.li1: How could the rep have handled the pricing objection better in this call?
coach.li2: What coaching feedback can you give on recent calls done by agent:josua?
coach.li3: Which sales person is performing well in their calls?
coach.tip: For coaching, anchor the analysis to a call, an agent, or a recent time window.

discovery.label: DISCOVERY TO DEMO
discovery.title: How to guide the conversation like a strong sales manager
discovery.q1: How do you review conversations today?
discovery.q2: How do you identify coaching opportunities?
discovery.q3: How do you understand objections today?
discovery.q4: How do you collect product feedback from calls?
discovery.q5: How do you detect recurring customer problems?
discovery.q6: How do reps prepare for meetings today?
discovery.q7: If you could ask your conversations one question, what would it be?
discovery.bridgeQuote: "Based on what you shared, let me show you how Ask Empower helps answer exactly those questions — but using the right call context so the output is actually useful."
discovery.bridgeSub: That last clause matters. It positions you as credible and reduces the risk of a weak open-ended demo.

comp.label: COMPETITOR FRAMING
comp.title: How to sell Ask Empower in front of competitors

notetakers.title: Note-takers
notetakers.li1: Great at summaries and meeting notes
notetakers.li3: Not built for broader intelligence and coaching workflows
notetakers.pitch: Note-takers summarize meetings. Ask Empower helps you understand conversations.

revenue.title: Revenue Intelligence
revenue.li1: Strong on revenue analytics and CRM-linked workflows
revenue.li2: Can require heavier setup and more operational discipline
revenue.li3: Often feel more enterprise and sales-only
revenue.pitch: Ask Empower makes conversational intelligence more accessible across teams, while building toward deeper revenue intelligence over time.

empower.title: Empower positioning
empower.li1: Built for coaching and insight, not just note-taking
empower.li2: Multi-team value: sales, support, recruiting, enablement
empower.li4: Works best when guided with context and filters
empower.li5: No recording required is a major differentiator

limits.label: LIMITATIONS TO SAY OUT LOUD
limits.title: What Ask Empower cannot do yet
limits.desc: The fastest way to lose credibility is to oversell this section.
limits.st2: Objection and pain-point analysis when filtered well
limits.bestLineText: "Today Ask Empower analyzes conversations to understand what happens inside deals. With CRM integration, it will move toward full revenue intelligence."

demo.label: DEMO TECHNIQUE
demo.title: The safest and strongest demo formula
demo.desc: Do not demo Ask Empower like a generic chatbot. Demo it like an intelligence engine that needs the right scope.
demo.formulaEx: Example: flag:Empower Demo → "What expectations are being set?" → "Buyers want clarity on setup, CRM fit, and coaching value" → "Now the manager knows what messaging to standardize."
demo.betterText: "In calls with moment:Objections Raised, what objections have been seen?"

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Basic questions sales will get
faq.q1: Is Ask Empower just a chatbot?
faq.a1: No. It is a conversational intelligence engine. It analyzes real conversations and surfaces insights, not just generic text responses.
faq.a2: Not in a fully exhaustive sense at query time. It analyzes relevant conversations or subsets to answer the question. That is why filtering matters.
faq.a3: Yes. It can work with Ringover, video meeting tools, third-party calling platforms, and uploaded conversations.
faq.a4: No. This is one of the strongest differentiators in regulated and compliance-heavy environments.
faq.q5: How much historical data can it use?
faq.a5: Use a maximum timeframe of up to 1 year.
faq.q6: Why do some broad questions feel weak?
faq.a6: Because the system may sample calls without enough context. Better prompts use flags, moments, accounts, agents, teams, and timeframes.

roadmap.label: ROADMAP
roadmap.title: What is coming next
roadmap.nextText: CRM integration with systems such as Salesforce and HubSpot.
roadmap.nextSub: This will allow Ask Empower to combine conversation signals with deal and pipeline context.
roadmap.visionSub: Conversations + CRM data + pipeline + forecasting. That is when questions like "Which deals are at risk?" become much more credible.

footer.text: Internal sales playbook for pitching Ask Empower
